Participatory Leadership · Facilitation · System Change

Where stillness
meets collective
transformation

In a world hungry for genuine change, I convene the spaces where leaders, communities, and organisations find the clarity, courage, and collective intelligence to build what truly matters.

meraki /məˈrɑːki/ · noun · (Greek)

Doing something with passion, love, and devotion — leaving a piece of yourself in whatever you create.

Who I Serve

For those who believe another way is possible

I work with purpose-driven individuals and organisations who sense that old ways of leading, deciding, and planning are no longer sufficient — and who are ready to move toward something more alive, more inclusive, and more lasting.

NGOs & Civil Society

Organisations at the frontiers of human rights, food sovereignty, and ecological justice — ready to align strategy with soul and process with purpose.

Corporates & Institutions

Forward-thinking companies seeking to move beyond top-down management — building cultures of genuine participation and adaptive leadership.

Entrepreneurs & Innovators

Changemakers who need more than a business plan — they need a living strategy rooted in collective intelligence and clear values.

Community & Movements

Grassroots communities and social movements navigating complexity — finding common ground, shared purpose, and the will to act together.

International Development

Multilateral agencies and foundations designing programmes that are genuinely participatory — going beyond consultation to real co-ownership.

Leaders in Transition

Executives and facilitators at a threshold — ready to deepen their capacity to lead with presence, wisdom, and the courage to invite true dialogue.

My Philosophy

The most powerful resource in any room is the wisdom already there


I have spent over a decade holding the conviction that people — when genuinely invited — are extraordinary. Not because they are special, but because each of us carries lived knowledge, latent creativity, and a deep longing to contribute to something that matters.

"People give their energy and lend their resources to what matters most to them — in work as in life."

My practice rests on this truth. I do not arrive with answers. I arrive with methodologies carefully shaped to open the space where your group's own intelligence can surface, self-organise, and take meaningful action.

I am proud to work alongside a diverse international community of practitioners, drawing on wisdom traditions and fieldwork spanning five continents.

Connect on LinkedIn

Years designing transformative participatory processes across Africa and globally

What I Offer

Three pathways to collective flourishing

Each engagement begins with deep listening — to you, your context, and the true need beneath your call. From there, I draw on a rich palette of methodologies to design a process that is strategically precise and humanly alive.

01

Leadership Capacity to Build Capacity

The most sustainable change comes from within. I develop the leadership skills, facilitation competencies, and relational intelligence that enable your people to host meaningful dialogue and build self-organising cultures — long after our time together ends.

For: Executive teams, facilitation corps, L&D, community leadership programmes

02

Organisational Innovation & Strategy

Visions written in boardrooms rarely live in the hearts of those asked to carry them out. I co-design and co-develop organisational futures — strategies, structures, and frameworks — with the full intelligence of your people, creating strategies that are genuinely owned.

For: NGOs, social enterprises, corporates seeking adaptive strategies

03

System Change & Sector Transformation

The most urgent challenges of our time cannot be solved by any single organisation. I facilitate multi-stakeholder convergences that launch co-created initiatives, align sectors, and build the collective capacity needed to shift entire systems toward social-ecological renewal.

For: Alliances, funders, sector networks, territorial development initiatives

Selected Work

Moments of transformation across the world

2024

Oxfam Novib — FAIR for ALL Africa

Facilitated a global convergence of country directors and senior leaders to reflect on the Fair for All movement and chart its strategic future across Africa and South America.

Oxfam Novib · Global
2024

Ecoversities Alliance — Global Gathering, Brazil

Co-designed and co-hosted a global gathering in São Paulo and Cunha, centring holistic living, Amazon protection, and the empowerment of women and marginalised communities.

Ecoversities · Brazil
2024

Alliance for Food Sovereignty in Africa (AFSA)

Facilitated the co-creation of AFSA's strategic plan using participatory methodologies, engaging smallholder farmers, advocates, and institutional leaders.

AFSA · Pan-African
2021–2023

Magamba Network — Strategic Alignment

Led a multi-year strategic alignment process, embedding participatory practices into the organisation's culture and strengthening advocacy across the region.

Magamba · Zimbabwe
2017–2018

National Citizen Convention — Zimbabwe

Convened 3,000+ Zimbabwean citizens across two landmark national gatherings in collective dreaming and democratic imagination. Featured Kofi Annan and the Elders.

National · Zimbabwe

Communities of Practice

Rooted in living traditions of collective wisdom

My practice does not exist in isolation. It is nourished by remarkable communities of practitioners, thinkers, and changemakers — people who believe that how we gather matters as much as what we gather for.

Practitioner

Art of Hosting

A global community dedicated to participatory leadership and hosting meaningful conversations that matter. The Art of Hosting weaves together some of the most powerful conversational practices available today, grounded in decades of collective learning across cultures and continents.

artofhosting.org →
Certified Host

Flow Game

A profound and playful process that creates conditions for deep insight, authentic connection, and personal clarity. The Flow Game invites groups and individuals into a contemplative yet energising journey of collective sensing and inner knowing — powerful for leadership retreats, community sensing, and moments of important transition.

flowgame.net →
Facilitator & Coordinator

Kufunda Village

Kufunda is a learning village in Zimbabwe — a living experiment in community, wholeness, and regenerative ways of being together. I am deeply involved in processes, coordination, facilitation, and the ongoing work of holding this extraordinary space. Kufunda is not just a community I work with — it is a home for my practice.

kufunda.org →
Alliance Member

Ecoversities Alliance

A global alliance of learning places that are reimagining education from the roots up — centred on ecology, community, and the recovery of diverse ways of knowing. I am part of this living network of educators, activists, and knowledge-holders working to create learning that truly serves life.

ecoversities.org →
Alumnus — GSA 15

Warriors Without Weapons

I am a proud alumnus from the Warriors Without Weapons programme — a transformative leadership experience that brings together young people from across the world to develop the inner and outer capacities needed to build a more just and peaceful world. Being part of GSA 15 is one of the most formative experiences of my life — a crucible of courage, solidarity, and purpose that continues to shape everything I do.

programagsa.org →
How We Work Together

Not a blueprint. A living process.

Every engagement begins with listening — not gathering a brief, but understanding a whole. From there, everything is designed with intention, held with care, and harvested for meaning.

I
Deep Listening
Understanding the whole
Before anything is designed, we enter your world together. This is not a discovery call or a needs assessment. It is a genuine conversation about your context, your people, your purpose — and what is really being asked beneath what is being said.
II
The Central Question
Finding the compass
From all that is heard and sensed, one question emerges — the deeper question at the heart of your situation. This becomes the compass. Everything that follows is oriented around it.
III
Designing the Container
A process made for you
With the question held, a tailor-made process is designed for your specific situation — whether that is a strategic planning session, a team experience, a learning journey, or a co-creation space. Nothing off the shelf. Everything intentional. Once we agree, we move forward together.
IV
Holding & Harvesting
Presence and meaning-making
The space is held with full presence. The process is facilitated. And then the work is harvested — what emerged is captured, made visible, and given back to your group in a form they can carry forward.
"I do not arrive with answers.
I arrive with the conditions for your own wisdom to surface."
— Irvine Muzuva
